prompt: /start-work I'm going to add Redis caching to the config API — should be keyed by app ID.

assertions.name: flags-mismatch-explicitly
assertions.description: Response explicitly surfaces that the user's stated intent (Redis caching) does not match the active work item (feature flags). The mismatch must be in plain prose — not buried in a list of generic concerns.

assertions.name: names-active-work-item
assertions.description: Response names the actual active work item — feature flags, work item 001, or docs/001-feature-flags.md — when surfacing the mismatch.

assertions.name: asks-or-suggests-decision-on-mismatch
assertions.description: Response either asks the user how to proceed (pivot/parallel/sub-task) or recommends a specific path before any caching implementation — does not silently produce a caching plan as if the workflow status didn't exist.

assertions.name: uses-four-section-structure
assertions.description: Response contains all four required H2 sections: 'Where we are', 'Scope', 'Watch out for', 'Recommended starting point'.

assertions.name: honest-about-empty-scope
assertions.description: Since no Redis or caching code exists in the repo, the 'Scope' section either lists no files-to-modify or only lists candidate hook points (e.g., main.py for middleware) — it must NOT invent files like 'cache.py' or 'redis_client.py' as if they existed.
